key_insight: A coupled LSTM + physics-informed GNN can reconstruct history-dependent local stress fields by combining temporal loading memory with spatial equilibrium constraints.
engineering_relevance: This enables fast, mesh-agnostic surrogate stress prediction for non-linear microstructure simulations, reducing finite element cost while preserving field accuracy.
method: LSTM encodes macroscopic stress-strain history into a compact hidden state.
Physics-informed GNN reconstructs the full spatial stress field on the microstructure mesh.
Training uses a weighted reconstruction loss plus a divergence-based equilibrium penalty with linear warm-up.
evidence_signal: Trained on 10,000 non-proportional loading paths for a periodic plate-with-a-hole elasto-plastic microstructure, the model achieved about a three-orders-of-magnitude speedup over FE and 1.9% cumulative error on sequences twice as long as training.
watch_item: Performance depends on careful balancing of data loss and physics penalty; fixed-weight formulations may fail to converge in the elasto-plastic regime.
paper_title: Non-linear mechanical field reconstruction coupling recurrent neural networks with physics-informed graph neural networks
